wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Test Plan Manager SaaS project overview. Explain the application's purpose as a comprehensive testing platform for test case management, automated test execution, and AI-powered test generation. Document the multi-platform architecture combining web and desktop applications built with Next.js, Electron, and TypeScript. Highlight key features including test case management, AI integration capabilities, real-time analytics, and external service integrations. Include the target audience, use cases, and competitive advantages. Provide architectural overview showing the clean architecture principles and domain-driven design approach. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Case Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Test Case Management feature. Document the hierarchical organization system including modules and test cases, priority management (P1-P4 levels), and import/export functionality. Explain the TestPlanService implementation, module creation and management, test case CRUD operations, and bulk import capabilities. Include detailed coverage of the data models, repository patterns, API endpoints for test case operations, and user workflows. Provide practical examples showing how to create modules, add test cases with different priorities, import test plans from various sources, and manage the complete test case lifecycle. Address configuration options, validation rules, and integration patterns with other system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clean Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the clean architecture overview section focused on the layered architecture design of Test Plan Manager. Explain the three main layers: Domain (business logic), Adapters (external integrations), and Infrastructure (persistence/configuration). Document how data flows from UI components through domain services to adapters and infrastructure. Describe the separation of concerns, dependency inversion principle, and how the architecture enables testability and maintainability. Include architectural diagrams showing layer relationships and component boundaries. Address the benefits of this architectural approach for a SaaS testing platform and how it supports both web and desktop deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for project management endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/projects, /api/projects/[id]), request/response schemas for project CRUD operations, and authentication requirements. Include detailed parameter specifications for project creation (name, description, settings), updates (partial updates), and deletion (cascade handling). Document return value structures, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address project ownership, permissions, and data validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships and Schema Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Test Plan Manager's database entity relationships and overall schema design. Explain the hierarchical relationship structure starting from Projects through Modules to TestCases, and how TestRuns connect to TestResults. Document the foreign key constraints, cascade deletion policies, and referential integrity rules. Include visual entity relationship diagrams showing table connections and data flow patterns. Explain the rationale behind the schema design choices, including the use of cuid2 for UUID generation and timestamp-based audit trails. Document the unique constraints (like the unique index on testRunId and testCaseId combination) and their business implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LLM provider integration in Test Plan Manager. Document the factory pattern implementation that dynamically creates LLM adapters based on configuration. Cover all supported providers: Gemini (default), OpenAI-compatible, Ollama (local), and OpenRouter. Include detailed setup instructions for each provider including API key configuration, base URL settings, and model selection. Document the ILLMProvider interface and how different adapters implement common functionality. Explain provider switching mechanisms, fallback strategies, and error handling. Include practical examples of provider configuration, troubleshooting common setup issues, and performance optimization tips for each provider type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Module Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Module Organization sub-feature. Explain the hierarchical structure system for organizing test cases within logical groupings called modules. Document the module creation process, module hierarchy management, and the relationship between modules and projects. Cover the IModuleRepository interface implementation, module persistence patterns, and the module lifecycle. Include practical examples of creating modules, organizing test cases hierarchically, and managing module relationships. Address module naming conventions, descriptions, and how modules serve as containers for test cases in the overall test management workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for shared UI components that serve as the foundation of the design system. Document the Button component with variants (primary, secondary, outline, ghost, danger) and sizes (sm, md, lg, icon), including prop specifications, styling classes, and usage examples. Cover the Input component with validation states, placeholder handling, and accessibility features. Detail the Card component for content containers, Modal component for dialog management, Badge component for status indicators, and StatusBadge component for test result states. For each component, provide code examples showing different variants and sizes, explain the styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes, document prop interfaces, event handling patterns, and integration with form validation. Include guidelines for extending components and maintaining design consistency across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Dashboard Components section. Document the DashboardCharts.tsx implementation including chart rendering patterns, data visualization components, and real-time update mechanisms. Explain the component architecture for displaying KPI cards, progress indicators, and interactive charts. Cover the integration with Recharts library, responsive design patterns, and state management for dashboard data. Include practical examples showing how to customize chart configurations, handle data loading states, and implement real-time dashboard updates. Address component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and styling approaches used throughout the dashboard interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Test Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Test Generation feature. Document the AITestGenerationService implementation including source code analysis, test plan creation workflow, and JSON response parsing. Explain the CodeFile interface, GeneratedTestCase structure, and GeneratedModule organization. Cover the system prompt engineering for QA engineers, user prompt construction from code files, and temperature/parameter tuning for deterministic outputs. Include practical examples showing how to generate test plans from JavaScript/TypeScript code, handle multiple file inputs, and process structured JSON responses. Address error handling for malformed JSON, provider configuration issues, and performance optimization for large codebases. Document the API endpoint integration and client-side usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Projects Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Projects table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key with cuid2 generation), name, description, and createdAt fields. Explain the business purpose of projects as containers for organizing test modules and runs. Detail the data validation rules, constraints, and default values. Include examples of typical project data and common operations like creation, updates, and deletion. Document the cascade delete behavior when projects are removed. Address performance considerations for project queries and filtering patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Run Lifecycle
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Test Run Lifecycle management. Document the complete lifecycle from test run creation to deletion, including the automatic result generation for existing test cases. Explain the TestRunService methods for creating runs with proper initialization, renaming runs for updates, and deleting runs with cascade cleanup. Cover the repository pattern implementation for test run persistence, API endpoint handling for run operations, and webhook dispatching for lifecycle events. Include practical examples showing run creation workflows, status management, and proper cleanup procedures. Address run validation, error handling for missing runs, and integration with the broader testing workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Gemini Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Gemini LLM provider integration. Document the GeminiAdapter implementation including API key configuration, model selection (gemini-pro, gemini-1.5-pro-latest, etc.), and request/response handling. Explain authentication setup, rate limiting considerations, and quota management. Include practical examples of Gemini API calls, prompt formatting requirements, and response processing. Document error handling strategies for common Gemini API issues, timeout handling, and retry mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guidance for authentication failures, quota exceeded errors, and network connectivity issues. Include performance optimization tips and best practices for Gemini-specific prompt engineering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Test Plan Manager. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js requirements and system dependencies. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation, environment variable setup (particularly GEMINI_API_KEY), and initial configuration. Document both web application development commands (npm run dev) and desktop application setup (npm run dev:electron). Include first-time user guidance for creating projects, adding test cases, and running test executions. Provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Execution Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Test Execution Management feature. Document the complete test run lifecycle including run creation, execution tracking, result recording, and completion reporting. Explain the TestRunService implementation, test result status management (PASSED, FAILED, BLOCKED, UNTESTED), progress monitoring, and real-time updates. Include comprehensive coverage of the test run data models, result tracking mechanisms, UI components for run monitoring, and API endpoints for run management. Provide practical examples showing how to create test runs, track execution progress, update test results, and generate completion reports. Address run scheduling, concurrent execution handling, and integration with the broader testing workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dependency Injection Container
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the dependency injection container implementation. Thoroughly explain the IoC container pattern used for service registration and resolution. Document the singleton pattern implementation using globalThis to prevent duplicate instances in Next.js App Router environment. Detail the service registration process for repositories, adapters, and domain services. Explain how the container manages dependencies between components and enables loose coupling. Include examples of service initialization, dependency resolution, and the lazy initialization strategy. Address the container's role in enabling testability through interface-based programming and how it supports the overall clean architecture principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Case Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for test case management endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/test-cases, /api/test-cases/[id]), request/response schemas for individual and bulk test case operations. Include detailed parameter specifications for test case creation (module association, priority P1-P4, steps, expected results), updates (hierarchical organization), and bulk operations (imports, exports). Document return value structures, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address test case relationships, validation rules, and import/export formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entities and Tables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the core database entities including Projects, Modules, TestCases, TestRuns, TestResults, and TestAttachments tables. For each entity, document field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules. Explain the business meaning of each field and its role in the testing workflow. Document the primary key strategies (cuid2 generation), audit fields (createdAt/updatedAt), and default values. Include examples of typical data stored in each table and common query patterns. Address indexing strategies and performance considerations for frequently accessed fields.

wiki_catalogs.name: Issue Tracker Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for issue tracker integration, focusing on Jira adapter implementation. Document the IIssueTracker interface and how JiraAdapter implements external issue creation and management workflows. Cover Jira authentication setup including API token configuration, server URL specification, and project mapping. Document issue creation workflows triggered by failed test cases, including issue template customization and field mapping. Include configuration examples, troubleshooting authentication issues, and integration testing procedures. Address security considerations for storing Jira credentials and best practices for issue synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Priority Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Priority Management sub-feature. Document the P1-P4 priority classification system for test cases, explaining the significance and impact levels associated with each priority tier. Cover how priorities are assigned during test case creation, imported from test plans, and managed throughout the test lifecycle. Include examples of priority assignment in different scenarios, priority-based filtering and sorting, and how priorities influence test execution strategies. Address the business logic behind priority classification, team workflows for priority management, and integration with test execution planning.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layout components that manage application structure and navigation. Document the Sidebar component including navigation items, collapsible behavior, active state management, and responsive design patterns. Cover the ProjectSwitcher component for project selection, dropdown functionality, and state synchronization. Detail the ThemeProvider component for theme management, dark/light mode switching, and CSS variable handling. Explain the integration between layout components and the overall application architecture, including state management through the project store. Provide examples of component composition, prop configurations, and customization options. Document accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation support, and responsive breakpoints. Include guidelines for extending the layout system and adding new navigation elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Analytics Data Models. Detail the DashboardData interface structure, ModuleStats model, and TestResult aggregation patterns. Explain the data transformation processes in DashboardService including status breakdown calculations, pass rate computations, and health score algorithms. Document the repository patterns for data retrieval, query optimization strategies, and caching mechanisms. Include examples of data flow from repositories to UI components, showing how raw database queries are transformed into dashboard-ready metrics. Address data validation, error handling, and performance considerations in data processing pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Bug Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Bug Reporting feature. Document the AIBugReportService implementation for generating structured bug reports from failed test executions. Explain the bug report template structure, failure analysis workflow, and integration with test result data. Cover prompt engineering for bug report generation, including reproduction steps, expected vs actual results, and severity assessment. Include practical examples showing how to process failed test cases, extract relevant debugging information, and create comprehensive bug reports. Address integration with existing test result tracking, report formatting options, and customization of bug report templates. Document error handling for incomplete test data and provider response issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modules Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Modules table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key), name, description, and projectId foreign key relationship to Projects. Explain the hierarchical organization concept where modules group related test cases within projects. Detail the cascade delete behavior when projects are removed. Include examples of module hierarchies and common query patterns for retrieving modules by project. Document validation rules, constraints, and indexing strategies for efficient module lookups.

wiki_catalogs.name: Result Tracking and Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for result tracking and status management within test executions. Detail the four status types (PASSED, FAILED, BLOCKED, UNTESTED) and their business meaning in the testing context. Explain the UpdateResultDTO structure, result update mechanisms, and real-time status changes. Document the TestResultRepository implementation for result persistence, UI components for result display and editing, and the integration between backend services and frontend interfaces. Include practical examples of updating test results, handling status transitions, and managing result notes. Address concurrent result updates, validation rules, and error scenarios in result tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: OpenAI-Compatible Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenAI-compatible LLM provider integration. Document the OpenAICompatibleAdapter implementation including base URL configuration, API key setup, and model parameter mapping. Explain how to configure endpoints for various OpenAI-compatible services (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Groq, etc.). Include detailed examples of different endpoint configurations, authentication headers, and model name mappings. Document request/response formatting, parameter validation, and error handling specific to OpenAI-compatible APIs. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common issues like incorrect base URLs, authentication failures, and model availability problems. Include performance optimization techniques and cost management strategies for OpenAI-compatible providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture and Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Test Plan Manager's clean architecture implementation. Describe the layered architecture with domain, adapters, and infrastructure layers. Document the dependency injection container pattern, service registration, and IoC implementation. Explain the repository pattern for data access abstraction, adapter pattern for external service integration, and factory pattern for dynamic LLM provider instantiation. Include component interaction diagrams showing data flow from UI components through domain services to adapters and infrastructure. Document design patterns implementation, architectural decisions, and system boundaries. Address cross-cutting concerns like event-driven architecture, error handling, and configuration management. Provide technical context for developers implementing extensions or modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dashboard and Analytics feature. Document the real-time dashboard components, analytics visualization using Recharts, performance metrics calculation, and trend analysis capabilities. Explain the DashboardService implementation, data aggregation patterns, chart rendering components, and real-time data updates. Include detailed coverage of the analytics data models, dashboard widgets, metric calculations, and state management integration. Provide practical examples showing how to configure dashboard views, interpret analytics data, track test performance trends, and export analytical reports. Address data refresh mechanisms, visualization customization, and integration with external analytics systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Repository Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the repository pattern implementation in Test Plan Manager. Explain how the repository pattern abstracts data access through interfaces, enabling separation between business logic and data storage concerns. Document the repository interfaces defined in the domain layer and their concrete implementations in the adapters layer. Detail the dual implementation approach with Drizzle ORM and in-memory repositories for testing. Include examples of repository method signatures, data mapping between domain entities and database records, and transaction management. Address how this pattern supports database independence, testability, and the overall clean architecture goals.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Run Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for test run management endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/runs, /api/runs/[id], /api/runs/[id]/finish, /api/runs/[id]/export, /api/runs/[id]/bug-report), request/response schemas for test run lifecycle management. Include detailed parameter specifications for run creation (test case selection, scheduling), result updates (status transitions PASSED/FAILED/BLOCKED/UNTESTED), completion handling, and export functionality. Document return value structures, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address run state management, progress tracking, and AI integration endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns and Repository Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data access patterns and repository implementation strategies used throughout the application. Explain how each repository implements CRUD operations, query optimization techniques, and transaction management. Document the repository pattern benefits, including abstraction of database operations and testability. Include examples of complex queries, joins, and aggregation operations performed by repositories. Address caching strategies, connection pooling, and performance optimization techniques. Explain error handling patterns and transaction boundaries across different operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for notification system integration, focusing on Slack notifier implementation. Document the INotifier interface and SlackNotifierAdapter implementation for sending test run alerts and progress updates. Cover Slack workspace configuration, bot token setup, channel selection, and message formatting. Document notification triggers including test run completion, failure alerts, and milestone achievements. Include webhook setup for incoming messages, permission configuration, and message customization options. Provide troubleshooting guides for common Slack integration issues, rate limiting considerations, and best practices for notification frequency and content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Import/Export Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Import/Export functionality sub-feature. Document the TestPlanService implementation for parsing Markdown test plans and bulk importing test cases. Explain the supported file formats, table structure requirements, and parsing algorithms used for extracting modules and test cases. Cover the import process workflow, duplicate detection and handling, and error management during bulk operations. Include practical examples of importing test plans from various sources, export capabilities for test case data, and integration with external testing tools. Address validation rules, data transformation processes, and best practices for maintaining data integrity during imports.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Test Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for specialized test management components. Document the TestRunHeader component for run metadata display, TestRunToolbar for action controls, TestResultRow for individual test result presentation, and TestResultDetail for detailed result views. Cover the AIBugReportButton for automated bug report generation and the AI-powered test design components (AIGenerateButton, ImportButton). For each component, explain the data binding patterns, state management integration, and user interaction workflows. Detail the component's role in the test execution lifecycle, including how they interact with domain services and repository patterns. Provide examples of component composition, prop requirements, event handling, and integration with the broader test management system. Include guidelines for extending test components and adding new functionality specific to test execution workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Metrics and Calculations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Performance Metrics and Calculations. Document the health score computation algorithm including weightings for pass rate, flaky tests, freshness, and coverage. Explain pass rate calculations, trend analysis formulas, and delta comparisons between time periods. Cover the mathematical models used for KPI calculations, statistical analysis of test results, and performance indicators. Include practical examples showing how metrics are calculated in real-time, how thresholds are determined, and how anomalies are detected. Address metric interpretation guidelines, benchmark establishment, and performance optimization recommendations based on dashboard insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the LLM Provider Integration system. Document the LLMProviderFactoryAdapter implementation and factory pattern for pluggable AI providers. Explain the adapter implementations for Gemini, OpenAI-compatible services, Ollama local deployment, and OpenRouter. Cover the ILLMProviderFactory and ILLMProvider interfaces, provider selection criteria, and configuration management. Include comprehensive setup guides for each provider including API key configuration, endpoint URLs, and authentication methods. Document provider-specific features, rate limiting considerations, cost optimization strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Address local vs cloud deployment options, performance characteristics, and troubleshooting provider connectivity issues. Provide examples of switching between providers and configuring environment-specific settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: TestCases Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the TestCases table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key), testId, title, steps, expectedResult, priority, and moduleId foreign key. Explain the test case structure with detailed fields: testId for external identifiers, steps for execution instructions, expectedResult for verification criteria, and priority levels (P1-P4). Detail the relationship to modules and the business logic behind test case organization. Include examples of well-formed test cases, priority assignment patterns, and common query operations for test case retrieval and filtering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress Monitoring and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the progress monitoring and analytics capabilities for test execution tracking. Explain the completion calculation logic including pass/fail/blocked/untested counts, severity determination based on execution results, and real-time progress indicators. Detail the TestRunHeader and TestRunToolbar components for visual progress display, the finishRun method implementation for completion processing, and notification systems for run completion. Include examples of progress calculation algorithms, severity scoring mechanisms, and integration with notification services. Address performance considerations for large test suites, real-time analytics updates, and completion reporting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ollama Local Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Ollama local LLM provider integration. Document the OllamaAdapter implementation including local server configuration, model installation and management, and API communication. Explain how to set up Ollama locally, install models (llama3, mistral, gemma2, etc.), and configure model parameters. Include practical examples of local model selection, inference parameters, and performance tuning. Document the adapter's handling of local model loading, memory management, and resource allocation. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common Ollama issues like model loading failures, GPU memory problems, and local server connectivity. Include best practices for local model management, disk space optimization, and performance monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core features of Test Plan Manager. Cover the four primary feature areas: Test Case Management (modules, priorities, imports), Test Execution Management (run lifecycle, result tracking), Dashboard and Analytics (real-time metrics, trend analysis), and AI-Powered Features (test generation, bug reporting). For each feature area, document the business logic, user workflows, data models, and integration patterns. Include practical examples from the codebase showing service implementations, API endpoints, and UI components. Explain the relationships between features and how they integrate to provide a cohesive testing workflow. Address common use cases, configuration options, and troubleshooting guidance.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI-Powered Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI-Powered Features section. Document the AI test generation from source code, AI bug report generation, LLM provider configuration, and prompt engineering templates. Explain the AITestGenerationService and AIBugReportService implementations, LLM provider factory pattern, multi-provider support (Gemini, OpenAI, Ollama, OpenRouter), and integration with external AI services. Include comprehensive coverage of AI service configuration, prompt templates, result processing, and error handling. Provide practical examples showing how to configure AI providers, generate test plans from code, create structured bug reports, and customize AI prompts. Address provider selection criteria, cost optimization, fallback mechanisms, and troubleshooting AI integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Adapter Pattern and External Integrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the adapter pattern implementation and external service integrations. Explain how the adapter pattern provides abstraction layers for external services like LLM providers, issue trackers, notification systems, and webhooks. Document the adapter interfaces defined in the domain layer and their concrete implementations. Detail the factory pattern used for dynamic LLM provider instantiation and how adapters handle external service configuration and authentication. Include examples of adapter method implementations, error handling strategies, and integration patterns. Address how this pattern enables pluggable integrations and supports the platform's extensibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AI integration endpoints. Document HTTP methods (POST), URL patterns (/api/ai/generate-plan), request/response schemas for AI-powered test plan generation. Include detailed parameter specifications for source code input, test coverage requirements, module organization, and AI provider configuration. Document return value structures with generated test plans, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address AI provider selection, prompt engineering, rate limiting considerations, and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Migrations and Schema Evolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the database migration strategy and schema evolution process for Test Plan Manager. Explain the Drizzle ORM configuration, migration file structure, and automated migration execution. Document the schema versioning approach, backward compatibility considerations, and rollback procedures. Include examples of adding new tables, modifying existing schemas, and handling data transformations during migrations. Address migration testing strategies, production deployment considerations, and database backup procedures. Explain how the application handles schema validation and initialization on first run.

wiki_catalogs.name: Webhook Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for webhook integration system in Test Plan Manager. Document the IWebhookDispatcher interface and WebhookDispatcherAdapter implementation for event-driven notifications to external systems. Cover webhook configuration including URL endpoints, authentication headers, payload formats, and retry mechanisms. Document supported event types including test run completion, result changes, and system status updates. Include webhook payload schemas, signature verification for security, and event filtering options. Provide examples of webhook consumers, testing procedures, and troubleshooting common delivery issues. Address webhook security best practices, rate limiting, and monitoring recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Case CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Test Case CRUD Operations sub-feature. Document the complete lifecycle of test case management including creation, reading, updating, and deletion operations. Explain the ITestCaseRepository interface, test case data models, and the relationship between test cases and modules. Cover the API endpoints for test case operations, request/response schemas, and validation rules. Include practical examples of creating test cases with different priorities, updating test case details, bulk operations, and soft/hard deletion strategies. Address test case relationships, dependencies, and integration with test execution workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for dashboard-specific components and data visualization elements. Document the DashboardCharts component including chart types, data formatting, interactive features, and responsive design considerations. Explain the integration with analytics data from the DashboardService, including data fetching patterns, loading states, and error handling. Cover chart configuration options, color schemes, tooltips, and drill-down functionality. Detail the component's role in presenting test analytics, performance metrics, and trend analysis. Provide examples of chart customization, data binding patterns, and integration with the overall dashboard system. Include guidelines for adding new chart types, implementing custom visualizations, and optimizing performance for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Aggregation and Real-time Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Data Aggregation and Real-time Updates. Document the parallel data fetching strategy using Promise.all, repository method implementations for historical data retrieval, and real-time update mechanisms. Explain the API endpoint implementation for dashboard data, caching strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Cover the data aggregation patterns for status breakdowns, trend analysis, and activity feeds. Include examples of efficient query patterns, data transformation workflows, and integration with the frontend state management. Address data consistency, update frequency, and handling of concurrent data modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Engineering and Templates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AI prompt engineering and template management. Document the system and user prompt construction for both test generation and bug reporting scenarios. Explain prompt optimization techniques, role-playing instructions for AI agents, and output format specifications. Cover context prompt customization, parameter tuning (temperature, max_tokens), and response validation strategies. Include practical examples of prompt templates for different programming languages, frameworks, and testing scenarios. Document best practices for prompt engineering including chain-of-thought reasoning, structured output formatting, and hallucination prevention. Address prompt versioning, A/B testing of prompts, and performance monitoring. Provide guidelines for customizing prompts for specific organizational needs and regulatory requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: TestRuns Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the TestRuns table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key), name, createdAt, updatedAt, and projectId foreign key. Explain the purpose of test runs as instances of test execution within a project context. Detail the timestamp management with automatic createdAt and updatedAt fields. Document the cascade delete behavior and the relationship to projects. Include examples of test run creation, lifecycle management, and common operations like run completion tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Run Management and Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for test run management and external integrations. Document the integration with notification systems (notifier interface) for run completion alerts, webhook dispatcher for external system communication, and bug report generation endpoints. Explain the notifier service integration for sending completion notifications via various channels, webhook event dispatching for external system synchronization, and export functionality for test run results. Include practical examples of configuring notification channels, setting up webhook endpoints, and integrating with external tools. Address security considerations for webhook authentication, notification delivery reliability, and integration troubleshooting.

wiki_catalogs.name: OpenRouter Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenRouter LLM provider integration. Document the OpenRouterAdapter implementation including API key configuration, model selection from the OpenRouter marketplace, and routing configuration. Explain how to authenticate with OpenRouter, browse available models, and select optimal models for different use cases. Include detailed examples of model routing, fallback strategies, and cost optimization techniques. Document the adapter's handling of model availability, rate limiting, and dynamic model selection. Provide troubleshooting guidance for OpenRouter-specific issues like API key problems, model unavailability, and routing failures. Include best practices for model selection, cost management, and performance optimization when using the OpenRouter ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in Test Plan Manager. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Cover project management APIs (CRUD operations), test case management APIs (bulk operations, imports), test run management APIs (lifecycle, results), AI integration APIs (test generation, bug reporting), and dashboard APIs (analytics, exports). Include detailed parameter specifications, return value documentation, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Document rate limiting, security considerations, and versioning information. Address common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer and Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the service layer and business logic implementation. Explain how domain services orchestrate business operations and coordinate between repositories, adapters, and domain models. Document the service interfaces and their implementations, focusing on how they encapsulate business rules and workflows. Detail the service responsibilities including test case management, test run execution, AI integration, and reporting. Include examples of service method implementations, business rule enforcement, and error handling strategies. Address how the service layer maintains business logic independence from external concerns and supports the overall clean architecture principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Analytics API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for dashboard analytics endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET), URL patterns (/api/dashboard), request/response schemas for analytics and statistics retrieval. Include detailed parameter specifications for data filtering (date ranges, project scope, test case categories), aggregation functions, and export formats. Document return value structures with performance metrics, trend analysis, and statistical summaries. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address data caching strategies, performance considerations, and real-time update mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: TestResults Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the TestResults table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key), status (with default UNTESTED), notes, updatedAt, testRunId foreign key, and testCaseId foreign key. Explain the status enumeration values (PASSED, FAILED, BLOCKED, UNTESTED) and their business meanings. Detail the unique index constraint preventing duplicate results for the same test case in a test run. Document the cascade delete behavior and the bidirectional relationships to test runs and test cases. Include examples of result tracking, status updates, and common query patterns for result analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provider Factory Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the LLM provider factory pattern implementation. Document the LLMProviderFactoryAdapter that dynamically creates and manages LLM provider instances based on configuration. Explain the factory pattern benefits, provider registration mechanisms, and runtime provider selection. Include practical examples of factory configuration, provider instantiation, and lifecycle management. Document the ILLMProviderFactory interface and how different adapters implement common functionality. Explain provider switching mechanisms, fallback strategies when providers fail, and load balancing approaches. Provide troubleshooting guidance for factory configuration issues, provider initialization failures, and runtime provider switching problems. Include best practices for factory configuration, performance optimization, and extensibility for adding new providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema and Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Test Plan Manager's database schema. Detail entity relationships, table structures, field definitions, and data types for Projects, Modules, TestCases, TestRuns, TestResults, and Attachments. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, constraints, and validation rules. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and data flow patterns. Explain data access patterns through repository implementations, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Document data lifecycle, retention policies, and migration management. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms. Include sample data examples and common query patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event-Driven Architecture and Event Bus
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the event-driven architecture and event bus implementation. Explain how the event system enables loose coupling between components through publish-subscribe patterns. Document the event types, event payload structures, and event handling mechanisms. Detail how events are triggered during test execution, result updates, and system operations. Include examples of event registration, event processing, and error handling in event handlers. Address the role of events in supporting notifications, webhooks, and integration patterns. Explain how the event system contributes to the overall decoupling strategy and supports the platform's extensibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attachment & File Upload API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for attachment and file upload endpoints. Document HTTP methods (POST, GET, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/attachments, /api/attachments/[id], /api/upload), request/response schemas for file upload and management. Include detailed parameter specifications for file upload (multipart/form-data, size limits, supported formats), metadata handling, and attachment retrieval. Document return value structures, error handling strategies for upload failures, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address security considerations, file validation, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: TestAttachments Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the TestAttachments table entity. Document the table structure including id (primary key), filePath, fileType, createdAt, and testResultId foreign key. Explain the purpose of attaching files (screenshots, logs, artifacts) to test execution results. Detail the file type validation and storage considerations. Document the cascade delete behavior when test results are removed. Include examples of attachment upload workflows, file management patterns, and common operations for retrieving and managing test evidence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integrations and External Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed integration documentation for external services and LLM providers in Test Plan Manager. Document the pluggable integration architecture using adapter pattern implementation. Cover LLM provider integrations including Gemini, OpenAI-compatible, Ollama, and OpenRouter configurations with setup instructions and API key management. Document issue tracker integration with Jira including authentication, project mapping, and issue creation workflows. Cover notification systems including Slack integration for test run alerts and progress updates. Document webhook configuration for external system notifications and event dispatching. Include troubleshooting guides for common integration issues, security considerations, and best practices for each integration type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings & Configuration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for settings and configuration endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT), URL patterns (/api/settings/integrations, /api/settings/clear), request/response schemas for system configuration and integration management. Include detailed parameter specifications for LLM provider setup, external service credentials, and system-wide settings. Document return value structures, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Address configuration validation, security considerations for sensitive data, and backup/restore procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Settings table entity. Document the table structure including key (primary key), value, and updatedAt fields. Explain the key-value pair configuration system used for storing application settings, integration credentials, and runtime configuration. Detail the string-based key naming convention and JSON serialization for complex values. Document common setting categories like AI provider configurations, external service integrations, and application preferences. Include examples of setting management operations and validation patterns for different setting types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Desktop Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Electron-based desktop application. Document the application architecture with main process and renderer process separation, IPC communication patterns, and security configurations. Explain window management, menu integration, and native OS features utilization. Cover file system integration, local storage management, and desktop-specific UI patterns. Document the build process using electron-builder, packaging configuration, and distribution strategies. Include development workflow for desktop app development, debugging techniques, and platform-specific considerations. Address security implications of desktop deployment, auto-updates, and sandboxing. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common desktop application issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components and Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UI component library and design system. Document reusable components including shared components (Button, Input, Modal, Card, Badge), layout components (Sidebar, ThemeProvider), and custom components (Dashboard charts, TestRun components). For each component, describe visual appearance, behavior, user interaction patterns, and prop specifications. Include usage examples with code snippets, accessibility compliance guidelines, and responsive design considerations. Document component composition patterns, styling approaches using Tailwind CSS, and theming support. Address component state management, event handling, and integration with the overall application architecture. Provide guidelines for extending the component library and maintaining design consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the state management architecture using Zustand and React Query. Document the global state store implementation, project state management, and query client integration for server state synchronization. Explain state persistence strategies, state update patterns, and subscription mechanisms. Cover the project store implementation for managing active project context and user preferences. Document the QueryProvider setup for React Query integration, caching strategies, and optimistic updates. Include practical examples of state usage in components, state hydration from server responses, and state cleanup patterns. Address performance considerations, memory management, and debugging techniques for state-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Environment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for application configuration and environment management. Document environment variable loading, configuration validation, and runtime configuration options. Explain the configuration hierarchy from environment variables to application defaults, including security considerations for API keys and sensitive data. Cover the dependency injection container configuration, service registration patterns, and configuration-driven service selection. Document Next.js configuration options, build optimizations, and deployment-specific settings. Include examples of different deployment environments (development, staging, production) and their configuration requirements. Address configuration validation, error handling for missing configurations, and troubleshooting configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation covering the multi-layered testing strategy. Document unit testing approaches for domain services and business logic, integration testing for repository implementations and API endpoints, and end-to-end testing using Playwright for complete user workflows. Explain the testing architecture, test data management, and mocking strategies for external dependencies. Cover schema validation testing, error handling verification, and performance testing approaches. Include test coverage requirements, continuous integration testing pipelines, and quality assurance processes. Document testing best practices, debugging techniques for test failures, and maintenance of test suites. Address testing of AI integrations, database operations, and desktop application functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for Test Plan Manager. Document the build process including Next.js optimization, TypeScript compilation, and asset bundling. Cover Docker configuration for containerized deployment, environment variable management, and volume mounting for persistent data. Explain the electron-builder setup for desktop application packaging, code signing, and distribution channels. Document CI/CD pipeline considerations, automated testing integration, and release management processes. Include deployment strategies for web applications, desktop applications, and hybrid deployment scenarios. Address monitoring, logging, backup strategies, and disaster recovery procedures. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and their solutions. Document error handling patterns, domain error types, and API error responses. Cover common setup issues including dependency problems, environment configuration errors, and database connectivity issues. Address performance troubleshooting for slow API responses, memory usage optimization, and database query optimization. Include debugging techniques for UI issues, state management problems, and desktop application issues. Document error monitoring, log analysis, and diagnostic tools. Provide FAQ sections covering frequently asked questions about installation, configuration, usage, and integration. Include escalation procedures for complex issues and support resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines for developers wanting to contribute to Test Plan Manager. Document the development workflow including fork and branch management, commit message standards, and pull request procedures. Cover code standards and linting requirements using ESLint and TypeScript configuration. Explain testing requirements, documentation standards, and review processes. Include setup instructions for development environment, debugging techniques, and local testing procedures. Document the project structure conventions, naming patterns, and architectural guidelines contributors must follow. Address issue reporting, feature request processes, and community interaction guidelines. Provide guidance for different types of contributions including bug fixes, feature additions, and documentation improvements.

wiki_items.title: Data Access Patterns and Repository Implementation

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Test Plan Manager - Comprehensive SaaS Testing Platform

## Project Introduction

**Test Plan Manager** is a sophisticated desktop-first SaaS testing platform designed to streamline test case management, automated test execution, and AI-powered test generation. The application combines modern web technologies with desktop application capabilities, offering both browser-based and native desktop experiences.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as a comprehensive solution for software testing teams, providing tools for test case design, execution tracking, result analysis, and AI-assisted test automation. It bridges the gap between traditional test management approaches and modern AI-driven development practices.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Unified Test Management**: Centralized platform for creating, organizing, and executing test cases
- **AI Integration**: Intelligent test case generation and bug reporting powered by multiple LLM providers
- **Multi-Platform Delivery**: Web-based interface with native desktop application capabilities
- **Extensible Architecture**: Modular design supporting various integrations (Jira, Slack, Webhooks)
- **Real-time Analytics**: Comprehensive dashboard with test health metrics and progress tracking

### Target Audience
- Software QA engineers and testers
- Development teams requiring robust test management
- Organizations seeking AI-assisted testing workflows
- Teams transitioning from manual to automated testing processes

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a clean architecture pattern with clear separation of concerns across multiple layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Electron Main Process] --> B[Next.js Web Server]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Domain Services]
    D --> E[Adapters]
    E --> F[Persistence Layer]
    
    G[React Frontend] --> H[UI Components]
    H --> I[State Management]
    I --> J[Domain Services]
    
    K[LLM Providers] --> E
    L[Issue Trackers] --> E
    M[Notification Systems] --> E
    
    F --> N[(SQLite Database)]
    F --> O[(File Storage)]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- React-based UI components with TypeScript
- Next.js App Router for routing and SSR
- Tailwind CSS for styling with custom theming
- Zustand for state management

**Backend Layer**
- Next.js API routes handling HTTP requests
- Domain services orchestrating business logic
- Adapter pattern for external integrations
- Repository pattern for data access

**Infrastructure Layer**
- Drizzle ORM for database operations
- Electron for desktop application capabilities
- File system for attachments and uploads
- Environment-based configuration management

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

1. **Inversion of Control (IoC)**: Container-based dependency injection
2. **Repository Pattern**: Abstract data access through interfaces
3. **Adapter Pattern**: External service integration abstraction
4. **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic LLM provider instantiation
5. **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven notifications and webhooks

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant UI
    participant Service
    participant Adapter
    participant Repository
    participant Database
    
    User->>+UI: Create Test Case
    UI->>+Service: validateAndCreate()
    Service->>+Adapter: generateTestCase()
    Adapter->>+Repository: save()
    Repository->>+Database: INSERT
    Database-->>-Repository: OK
    Repository-->>-Adapter: TestCase
    Adapter-->>-Service: Generated Case
    Service-->>-UI: Result
    UI-->>-User: Success
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Electron Application Entry**
- `electron/main.ts` - Desktop application bootstrap with window management and IPC communication
- `electron/preload.ts` - Secure context isolation for renderer process

**Web Application Entry**
- `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with theme provider and sidebar integration
- `app/page.tsx` - Main dashboard with real-time analytics and interactive components

### Core Modules

**Dependency Injection Container**
- `src/infrastructure/container.ts` - Centralized service registration and dependency resolution
- Implements singleton pattern with globalThis for Next.js compatibility

**Domain Services**
- `src/domain/services/TestRunService.ts` - Test execution lifecycle management
- `src/domain/services/AITestGenerationService.ts` - AI-powered test case generation
- `src/domain/services/AIBugReportService.ts` - Automated bug reporting system

**Repository Implementations**
- `src/adapters/persistence/drizzle/` - SQLite-based data persistence layer
- Multiple repository classes for Projects, Test Cases, Test Runs, and Results

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `src/infrastructure/config.ts` - Typed configuration with environment variable support
- Supports multiple LLM providers (Gemini, OpenAI, Ollama, OpenRouter)
- Database and storage path configuration

**Build Configuration**
- `package.json` - Comprehensive script definitions for development, build, and packaging
- `next.config.ts` - Next.js optimization and build configuration
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript compilation settings

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**
- `next`: React-based web framework with App Router
- `react` & `react-dom`: Frontend UI library
- `electron`: Desktop application framework
- `drizzle-orm`: Type-safe database ORM

**Development Tools**
- `@playwright/test`: End-to-end testing framework
- `eslint` & `typescript`: Code quality and type safety
- `tailwindcss`: Utility-first CSS framework

### Integration Points

**AI/LLM Integration**
- `src/adapters/llm/LLMProviderFactoryAdapter.ts` - Dynamic provider selection
- Support for multiple AI providers with unified interface
- Configurable model selection and API key management

**External Service Integrations**
- `src/adapters/issue-tracker/JiraAdapter.ts` - Jira issue creation and updates
- `src/adapters/notifier/SlackNotifierAdapter.ts` - Real-time notifications
- `src/adapters/webhook/WebhookDispatcherAdapter.ts` - Event-driven integrations

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[UI Components] --> B[Domain Services]
    B --> C[Repository Interfaces]
    C --> D[Concrete Repositories]
    D --> E[Database Layer]
    
    F[Adapters] --> G[External APIs]
    H[Factories] --> F
    
    I[Configuration] --> J[Container]
    J --> B
    J --> F
    J --> H
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Test Case Management**
- Hierarchical test case organization with modules and priorities
- Import/export functionality for test case data
- Version control and change tracking

**Test Execution Tracking**
- Real-time test run monitoring with progress indicators
- Automated result categorization (PASSED, FAILED, BLOCKED, UNTESTED)
- Historical trend analysis and pass rate calculations

**AI-Powered Features**
- Intelligent test case generation from natural language descriptions
- Automated bug report creation with AI assistance
- Smart test prioritization based on historical data

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Dashboard**
- `app/page.tsx` - Interactive dashboard with live data updates
- Custom chart components for analytics visualization
- Auto-refresh mechanism with manual override capability

**Modular Architecture**
- `src/infrastructure/state/` - Centralized state management
- `src/ui/` - Reusable component library
- `src/domain/` - Business logic encapsulation

**Desktop Application Capabilities**
- `electron/main.ts` - Native desktop window management
- File system integration for local storage
- Cross-platform build support (Windows, macOS, Linux)

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ProjectSelection
    ProjectSelection --> TestCaseDesign: Create Project
    TestCaseDesign --> TestExecution: Import/Generate Cases
    TestExecution --> ResultAnalysis: Execute Runs
    ResultAnalysis --> AIEnhancement: Analyze Results
    AIEnhancement --> TestCaseDesign: Improve Cases
    AIEnhancement --> BugReporting: Report Issues
    BugReporting --> ResultAnalysis: Track Fixes
    ResultAnalysis --> ProjectSelection: New Project
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Database indexing for frequently queried tables
- Lazy loading for large datasets
- Efficient caching strategies for dashboard data
- Optimized API responses with pagination support

**Security Features**
- Input validation and sanitization
- Secure file upload handling
- Environment-based configuration management
- Electron security best practices

**Scalability Architecture**
- Microservice-ready domain boundaries
- Pluggable adapter pattern for external integrations
- Configurable database providers
- Stateless API design for horizontal scaling

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [container.ts](src/infrastructure/container.ts)
- [TestRunService.ts](src/domain/services/TestRunService.ts)
- [withApiHandler.ts](app/api/_lib/withApiHandler.ts)
- [LLMProviderFactoryAdapter.ts](src/adapters/llm/LLMProviderFactoryAdapter.ts)
- [main.ts](electron/main.ts)
- [schema.ts](src/infrastructure/db/schema.ts)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [route.ts](app/api/projects/route.ts)
- [route.ts](app/api/runs/route.ts)
- [config.ts](src/infrastructure/config.ts)

</blog>
